chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْأَوْقَاتِ وَ الْحَالَاتِ الَّتِي تُرْجَى فِيهَا الْإِجَابَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – The timings and the situation in which the Answer is hoped for

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/34)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/34)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يُسْتَجَابُ الدُّعَاءُ فِي أَرْبَعَةِ مَوَاطِنَ فِي الْوَتْرِ وَ بَعْدَ الْفَجْرِ وَ بَعْدَ الظُّهْرِ وَ بَعْدَ الْمَغْرِبِ .

en.hubeali: From him, from his father and someone else, from Al Qasim Bin Urwat, from Abu Al Abbas Fazl Al Baqbaaq who said,
‘Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘A supplication is Answered in four places – during Al-Witr, and after Al-Fajr, and after Al-Zohr and after Al-Maghrib (Salāts)’.
en.sarwar: 2. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from his father from others from al-Qasim ibn ‘Urwah from abu al-‘Abbas Fadl al-Baqbaq who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘One’s praying and pleading before Allah for help is accepted in four occasions: in al-Witr, the last Rak’at of night prayer, after dawn, after noon time and after sunset.”’
